mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals increasing inflationary pressure, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy by raising short-term interest rates, which pushes up the 10-year Treasury yield as market participants front-run future rate hikes.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for CPIAUCSL monthly changes vs 20-day forward yield change is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or directional accuracy remains below 55% in a consistent sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above a 4.5% threshold, it signals labor market weakening and rising recession risk, which increases demand for safe-haven Treasury assets and pushes yields down over a short horizon.
failure_condition: In at least 10 triggered events, the 5-day forward yield change is positive in more than 60% of cases, or the average yield change is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and rises in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicate a weakening domestic demand and deteriorating labor market, which amplifies recession fears and drives investors into Treasuries, causing yields to fall over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month declines > 5% and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: The composite interaction signal (HOUST decline and UNRATE rise) produces a Rank IC below 0.05 or a negative hit ratio over any 5-year period in out-of-sample walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Rises in industrial production (INDPRO) only lead to higher 10-year yields when the economy is in a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS above 4% and rising), because stronger supply is interpreted as adding to overheating and inflation risk, forcing yields up.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS above 4% and rising over past 3 months)
failure_condition: In months classified as tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS>4% and rising), the rolling 12-month Beta of 20d yield changes to INDPRO surprises is not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96) or the regime-conditional IC does not exceed 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, which drives up prices and inflation expectations, leading to higher longer-term interest rates as the market anticipates tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for RSAFS 1-month changes vs 20-day forward yield changes is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) crosses above 4.50%, it signals the Fed is firmly in hiking mode to combat inflation, which prompts markets to reprice the entire term structure higher, pushing the 10-year yield up over a longer horizon as the tightening cycle unfolds.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following each trigger, the 10-year yield declines in more than 40% of cases, or the average cumulative change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly indicates a strong economy that risks overheating, reinforcing the Phillips curve mechanism; this pairing pushes long-term yields up faster than either factor alone as the market prices in aggressive tightening.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising > 25bp over 3 months AND UNRATE falling > 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS rise * UNRATE fall) does not produce a positive average cumulative yield change over the following 20 trading days when tested on out-of-sample data, or the interaction IC is below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market regime (UNRATE below 4.0%), rising housing starts (HOUST) further intensify labor demand and wage pressures, which exacerbates inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield up; outside this regime the effect is weak or negative.
failure_condition: In the subset of observations where UNRATE < 4.0%, the 5-day forward yield change following rising HOUST is negative more than 50% of the time, or the mean yield change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
